evidence: Shard review supports existing USP-RAW-001: content scripts run in all frames; any frame video-context records that frame as preferred and updates tab-level media state; desktop commands route to the preferred frame.

method: Full-file review plus focused vitest evidence
evidence: Focused command passed. Static review found browser-derived tabId prevents content-script cross-tab spoofing, but no top-frame/focused-frame/user-activation check exists before preferred frame replacement or desktop command routing.
proof_gap: No browser harness was run with competing top-frame and iframe videos; validation should reproduce whether a hidden or third-party frame can win preferred-frame state under real media event timing.

source: Third-party or nested frame with a video inside a tab where the extension is active
broken_control: Per-tab state and lastFrameId are updated by any frame, then reused as the command target
sink: DesktopMessageHandler posts seek/loop/play/pause/stopLoop controls to the preferred frame port
impact: Frame can replace the tab desktop-visible media context, leak frame URL/title/media metadata to desktop, trigger subtitle loading, and receive desktop controls intended for the top-level media
preconditions: Content script is injected into the frame
Frame emits video-context or loadedmetadata/play events for qualifying media
severity_facts: Threat model classifies extension command routing across tab/frame boundaries as medium impact
Cross-tab spoofing is mitigated because tabId comes from port.sender.tab.id
